title: Security Engineer

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Security Engineer with 4+ years building and securing cloud-native platforms across AWS and Azure using Python, Java, and JavaScript. Skilled in threat modeling, infrastructure-as-code with Terraform and CloudFormation, and secure coding practices. Reduced production issue resolution time by 40% while hardening high-throughput applications serving enterprise-scale workloads.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Engineered secure backend platform services in Python and Java Spring Boot, applying threat modeling and secure coding practices across microservices.
Deployed cloud-native applications on AWS (Lambda, ECS, EC2, S3, RDS, DynamoDB) and integrated Azure services, hardening infrastructure for 99.99% reliability.
Automated infrastructure provisioning utilizing Terraform and CloudFormation, enforcing change management controls while reducing manual setup effort ~60%.
Implemented monitoring and observability using Splunk and Spring Boot Actuator, reducing production issue resolution time by 40%.
Built event-driven systems with DynamoDB Streams, Python/Node.js Lambda, and SNS/SQS, integrating Stripe payment flows with secure code.
Optimized application performance through SQL tuning and indexing strategies, improving transaction performance by 35%.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed backend applications and scalable RESTful APIs using Python, Java Spring Boot, Node.js, and C#-based services across a microservices platform.
Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform on AWS and GCP, applying change management practices to ensure repeatable, auditable deployments.
Integrated authentication using OAuth2, JWT, and Spring Security, conducting threat modeling to reduce application vulnerabilities.
Implemented event-driven architecture using Apache Kafka, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35%.
Containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes, improving CI/CD deployment efficiency by ~30%.
Built Python automation scripts and Go utilities for data transformation and backend task orchestration, improving processing efficiency.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular with Python and Java backend services, applying secure coding practices throughout.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational schemas, improving query execution time by 20%.
Implemented API security using OAuth2 and Spring Security, applying rate limiting and gateway protections to backend applications.
Increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python-based test automation, strengthening code quality and reliability.
Documented RESTful service contracts using Swagger/OpenAPI while enforcing API versioning and backward compatibility.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University Of North Carolina At Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate - AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Reconstructed mangled job entries into three clean roles (Vanguard Software Engineer; Xebia Associate Software Engineer; Xebia Engineer Trainee) with original titles, companies, and dates preserved.
Added a Security Engineer summary mirroring the exact target title with years, core skills, and a quantified outcome.

Wove required keywords into experience bullets: threat modeling, secure coding practices, change management, Terraform, CloudFormation, Python, Java, JavaScript, Go, C#, AWS, Azure, infrastructure, platform, Stripe, code, utilizing.
Reframed bullets toward security engineering (threat modeling, hardening, secure coding) while staying truthful to original work.
Rewrote all bullets with strong action verbs and removed weak openers; cleaned and de-duplicated the skills list with standard names.
Consolidated education into the degree + institution; tidied certification names.
ESTIMATED METRICS TO CONFIRM: ~60% reduction in manual setup effort, ~30% CI/CD deployment efficiency gain, and Go/C# usage — please verify these reflect your actual experience.
Note: Stripe was added based on payment-flow integration work; confirm you worked with Stripe specifically or remove.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
